Also, removing one ring methyl or modifying the piperidine ring to reduce flexibility might help SA slightly.

I propose replacing the para-CF3 on the phenyl ring with a cyano, and replacing the morpholine ring with a simpler oxazolidine ring to maintain hydrogen bonding but reduce complexity.

Proposed molecule SMILES: CC1CCN(c2ccc(C#N)cc2C(=O)N[C@@H]2COCN2)CC1

This molecule keeps the biaryl amide core with the chiral oxazolidine ring, replacing morpholine to reduce complexity and the CF3 to CN to potentially improve binding and QED. This is expected to have a QED slightly higher (>0.88), SA slightly lower (<3), and binding affinity better (more negative) than -8.72 due to improved H-bond interactions.

The new molecule SMILES would be: COc1ccc(O)cc1NS(=O)(=O)c1cncnc1

This is likely to maintain or slightly improve QED (due to similar size and moderate polarity), have a similar or better SA (small groups, easy to synthesize), and improve binding affinity by adding extra H-bond donors and acceptors.

NC(=O)c1ccc(Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2)cc1 — no carbonyl in original.

Original: Oc1ccc(Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2)cc1

So the replacement:  NC#Cc1ccc(Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2)cc1

Better: N#Cc1ccc(Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2)cc1  (para-cyano)

But must retain the hydroxyl (O) in phenyl - swapping O for CN is a big change. Instead, replace the hydroxyl with CN to improve affinity but might harm QED.

Alternative: Add CN at para position and keep OH at ortho or meta to balance polarity.

Easier substitution: replace the para-hydrogen with CN in phenyl ring while keeping OH at ortho:

SMILES for 2-hydroxy-4-cyanophenyl-amino quinazoline core:

OCc1cc(C#N)cc1Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2

To correctly represent, put OH at position 2, CN at position 4 on phenyl attached to aniline N:

SMILES: Oc1cc(Nc2ncc3ccccc3n2)cc(C#N)c1
